revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''宇内青津古墳群'''（うないあおつこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[会津坂下町]]にある、16基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。 ...'
revision.text:
'''宇内青津古墳群'''（うないあおつこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[会津坂下町]]にある、16基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。
== 概要 == [[前方後円墳]]13基と[[前方後方墳]]3基が残る全国有数の大型古墳群である。
== 主な古墳 == * [[亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳]] - 国の史跡。亀ヶ森古墳は墳長127メートルで県下最大。 * [[杵ガ森古墳]] - 東北地方最古級の古墳。4世紀初頭に築造。 * 臼ガ森古墳 * 長井前ノ山古墳 * 虚空蔵森古墳 * 天神免古墳 * 灰塚山古墳 * 宮東1号墳 * 雷神山1号墳 * 森北1号墳 * 鍛冶山4号墳 このほか古墳群内には多数の周溝墓群が確認されている。
この古墳群の主要古墳のほとんどは前期古墳と考えられる。古墳時代前期を通じて古墳が継続的に築造された地域は、大和などの中枢を除けば列島内を見渡してもほとんどない。
== 参考文献 == 辻秀人『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029 新泉社 2006年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うないあおつこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:福島県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:会津坂下町の歴史]]

'''宇内青津古墳群'''（うないあおつこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[会津坂下町]]にある、16基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群。
== 概要 == [[前方後円墳]]13基と[[前方後方墳]]3基が残る全国有数の大型古墳群である。
== 古墳 == * [[亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳]] - 国の史跡。亀ヶ森古墳は墳長127メートルで県下最大。 * [[杵ガ森古墳]] - 東北地方最古級の古墳。4世紀初頭に築造。 * 臼ガ森古墳 * 長井前ノ山古墳 * 虚空蔵森古墳 * 天神免古墳 * 灰塚山古墳 * 宮東1号墳 * 雷神山1号墳 * 森北1号墳 * 鍛冶山4号墳 * 出崎山1号墳 * 出崎山2号墳 * 出崎山3号墳 * 出崎山4号墳 このほか古墳群内には多数の周溝墓群が確認されている。
この古墳群の主要古墳のほとんどは前期古墳と考えられる。古墳時代前期を通じて古墳が継続的に築造された地域は、大和などの中枢を除けば列島内を見渡してもほとんどない。
== 参考文献 == 辻秀人『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029 新泉社 2006年

revision.comment: /* 概要 */ 国郡制成立以前の古墳時代の地域名称に「国」を付すべきではない。
revision.text:

== 概要 == [[前方後円墳]]13基と[[前方後方墳]]3基が残る全国有数の大型古墳群である。この古墳群の主要古墳のほとんどは前期古墳と考えられる。[[古墳時代]]前期を通じて古墳が継続的に築造された地域は、[[大和国|大和]]（現在の[[奈良県]]）などの中枢を除けば列島内を見渡してもほとんど存在しない。
== 古墳 == * [[亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳]] - 国の史跡。亀ヶ森古墳は墳長127メートルで県下最大。 * [[杵ガ森古墳]] - 東北地方最古級の古墳。4世紀初頭に築造。 * 臼ガ森古墳 * 長井前ノ山古墳 - 合掌天井式石棺出土。5世紀前半の築造。 * 虚空蔵森古墳 * 天神免古墳 * 灰塚山古墳 * 宮東1号墳 * 雷神山1号墳 * 森北1号墳 * 鍛冶山4号墳 * 出崎山1号墳 * 出崎山2号墳 * 出崎山3号墳 * 出崎山4号墳 このほか杵ガ森古墳と臼ガ森古墳の間に、米ガ森古墳があったと伝えられている。
== 参考文献 == * 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029、新泉社、2006年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うないあおつこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:会津坂下町の歴史]]

It consists of 16 burial mounds. == Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳}}, making it one of..."
revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It consists of 16 burial mounds.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori and Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Kofun groups]] [[ja:宇内青津古墳群]]

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It consists of 16 burial mounds.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It consists of 16 burial mounds.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It consists of 16 burial mounds.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It consists of 16 burial mounds.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It consists of 16 burial mounds.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It consists of 16 burial mounds.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "単一の出典" to "One source"
revision.text:
{{One source|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}. It consists of 16 burial mounds.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mounds|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}}

Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=draft}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=draft}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{One source|date=2013rd year 10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=draft}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|ja|会津坂下町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}. It consists of 16 Kofuns.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofuns|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|ja|亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳|12=draft}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=draft}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling stone coffin, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=draft}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{citation needed|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group}} <!--Unai Aotsu Kofun Group-->

revision.text:
{{単一の出典|date=2013年10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''宇内青津古墳群'''（うないあおつこふんぐん）は、[[福島県]][[会津坂下町]]にある、16基の[[古墳]]からなる古墳群である。
== 概要 == [[前方後円墳]]13基と[[前方後方墳]]3基が残る全国有数の大型古墳群である。この古墳群の主要古墳のほとんどは前期古墳と考えられる。[[古墳時代]]前期を通じて古墳が継続的に築造された地域は、[[大和国|大和]]（現在の[[奈良県]]）などの中枢を除けば列島内を見渡してもほとんど存在しない。
== 古墳 == * [[亀ヶ森・鎮守森古墳]] - 国の史跡。亀ヶ森古墳は墳長127メートルで県下最大。 * [[杵ガ森古墳]] - 東北地方最古級の古墳。4世紀初頭に築造。 * 臼ガ森古墳 * 長井前ノ山古墳 - 合掌天井式石棺出土。5世紀前半の築造。 * 虚空蔵森古墳 * 天神免古墳 * [[灰塚山古墳]] * 宮東1号墳 * 雷神山1号墳 * 森北1号墳 * 鍛冶山4号墳 * 出崎山1号墳 * 出崎山2号墳 * 出崎山3号墳 * 出崎山4号墳 このほか杵ガ森古墳と臼ガ森古墳の間に、米ガ森古墳があったと伝えられている。
== 参考文献 == * 辻秀人 『東北古墳研究の原点・会津大塚山古墳』 シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」029、新泉社、2006年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うないあおつこふんくん}} [[Category:福島県の古墳]] [[Category:会津坂下町の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]]

== Overview == The group includes 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], very few regions aside from the central areas such as [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling Sarcophagus, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|part_of={{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710}}; {{ill|Kinegamori-kofun|WD=Q17211679}}; {{ill|Q28683863|WD=Q28683863}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17217360]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=2013rd year 10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It consists of 16 Kofuns.
== Overview == The group includes 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], very few regions aside from the central areas such as [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling Sarcophagus, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling Sarcophagus, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|part_of={{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710}}; {{ill|Kinegamori-kofun|WD=Q17211679}}; {{ill|Q28683863|WD=Q28683863}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17217360]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=2013rd year 10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It consists of 16 Kofuns.
== Overview == The group includes 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], very few regions aside from the central areas such as [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling Sarcophagus, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|宇内青津古墳群|version=60992243|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:kofun clusters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]]
}}
[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]]
<!--Unai Aotsu Kofun Group--> [[Category:Municipal History of Aizubange]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukushima prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17217360}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|part_of={{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710}}; {{ill|Kinegamori-kofun|WD=Q17211679}}; {{ill|Q28683863|WD=Q28683863}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17217360]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{One source|date=2013rd year 10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in [[:en:Aizubange|Aizubange]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It consists of 16 Kofuns.
== Overview == The group includes 13 [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], very few regions aside from the central areas such as [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (modern-day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * [[:en:Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun]] - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling Sarcophagus, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|part_of={{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710}}; {{ill|Kinegamori-kofun|WD=Q17211679}}; {{ill|Q28683863|WD=Q28683863}}}} {{One source|date=2013rd year 10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|en|Aizubange|WD=Q1204988|lt=Aizubange}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It consists of 16 Kofuns.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|en|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710|lt=Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling Sarcophagus, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|宇内青津古墳群|version=60992243|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宇内青津古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|part_of={{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710|1=Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun}}; {{ill|Kinegamori-kofun|WD=Q17211679|1=Kinegamori Kofun}}; {{ill|Q28683863|WD=Q28683863|1=Hazukayama Kofun}}}} {{One source|date=2013rd year 10月21日 (月) 08:09 (UTC)}} '''Unai Aotsu Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|ja|宇内青津古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unai Aotsu Kofun Group|qq=}}) is a {{ill|kofun group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}} located in {{ill|Aizubange|en|Aizubange|WD=Q1204988|lt=Aizubange|1=Aizubange}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It consists of 16 Kofuns.
== Overview == The group includes 13 {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} and 3 {{ill|rectangular mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, making it one of the most significant large-scale kofun groups in Japan. Most of the primary mounds in this group are considered early-period kofun. Throughout the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, very few regions aside from the central areas such as {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} (modern-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) experienced sustained kofun construction.
== Notable Kofun == * {{ill|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|en|Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|WD=Q11370710|lt=Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun|1=Kamegamori-Chinjumori Kofun}} - A National Historic Site. Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling Sarcophagus, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
== References == * Hidehito Tsuji, *The Foundation of Tohoku Kofun Research: Aizu Otsukayama Kofun*, "Learning from Historical Sites" Series 029, Shinseisha, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}}

Kamegamori Kofun, at 127 meters in length, is the largest in the prefecture. * {{ill|Kinegamori Kofun|ja|杵ガ森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinegamori Kofun|qq=}} - Among the oldest kofun in the Tohoku region, constructed in the early 4th century. * Usagamori Kofun * Nagaimae no Yama Kofun - Features a gabled ceiling Sarcophagus, constructed in the early 5th century. * Kokuzomori Kofun * Tenjinmen Kofun * {{ill|Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Hazukayama Kofun|lt=Haizukayama Kofun|ja|灰塚山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haizukayama Kofun|qq=}} * Miyahigashi No. 1 Kofun * Raijinzan No. 1 Kofun * Morikita No. 1 Kofun * Kajiyama No. 4 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 1 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 2 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 3 Kofun * Dezakiyama No. 4 Kofun
Additionally, it is said that a kofun called Maigamori Kofun existed between Kinegamori and Usagamori Kofun.
